heading: Army Medical Department
label: 7067 Army Medical Department
notes: Historical and Revision Notes1956ActRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)306710:81–1 (less last sentence).(less last sentence), .June 28, 1950, ch. 383, § 30764 Stat. 270The words “authorized by sections 21f and 21h, respectively, of this title” are omitted as surplusage. 10:81–1 (2d sentence) is omitted as covered by .section 3064 of this title
1958ActRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)3067(8)10 App.:81–1., .Aug. 9, 1955, ch. 654, § 3(a)69 Stat. 579

Amendments2018— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–232section 3067 of this title1968— substituted “Army Medical Department” for “Army Medical Service”.Pub. L. 90–3291958— substituted “Army Medical Specialist Corps” for “Women’s Medical Specialist Corps” in cl. (8).Pub. L. 85–861

Effective Date of 2018 AmendmentAmendment by effective , with provision for the coordination of amendments and special rule for certain redesignations, see , set out as a note preceding .Pub. L. 115–232section 800 of Pub. L. 115–232section 3001 of this titleFeb. 1, 2019

Augmentation of Army Medical Department by Detailing Reserve Officers of Public Health Service, title VII, § 755(a), (b)], , , 1654A–197, provided that:Pub. L. 106–398, § 1 [[div. A]114 Stat. 1654Oct. 30, 2000“(a).—AuthorityThe Secretary of the Army and the Secretary of Health and Human Services may jointly conduct a program to augment the Army Medical Department by exercising any authorities provided to those officials in law for the detailing of reserve commissioned officers of the Public Health Service not in an active status to the Army Medical Department for that purpose.“(b).—AgreementThe Secretary of the Army and the Secretary of Health and Human Services shall enter into an agreement governing any program conducted under subsection (a).”
